prompt_text:
Analyze the following java source code for violations of the Single Responsibility Principle (SRP).

Use a TWO-STAGE reasoning process:

## Stage 1 — List design smells (silent, for your own reasoning)
Identify relevant low-level design smells you observe in the code:
- God Class, Feature Envy, Shotgun Surgery, Divergent Change
- Long Method, Large Class, Long Parameter List
- Refused Bequest, Inappropriate Intimacy, Message Chains
- Switch Statements on type, Data Clumps
Think through these int...
raw_response:
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/Event.java",
      "entity_name": "Event",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 58,
      "line_end": 450,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "The Event class handles data storage, serialization, string interpolation, and memory estimation.",
      "reasoning": "God Class smell -> SRP violation because the class encapsulates multiple unrelated responsibilities: it acts as a data carrier (ConvertedMap), handles complex I/O concerns (JSON/CBOR serialization in toJson/fromJson), implements business logic (tagging/timestamp parsing), and provides utility functions like string interpolation (sprintf) and resource management (estimateMemory)."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/execution/AbstractPipelineExt.java",
      "entity_name": "AbstractPipelineExt",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 100,
      "line_end": 700,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "AbstractPipelineExt manages pipeline execution, plugin lifecycle, instrumentation, and external resource integration.",
      "reasoning": "God Class smell -> SRP violation because it centralizes too many distinct concerns. It manages the pipeline execution state, acts as a coordinator for plugin instantiation (PluginFactory), handles instrumentation and flow metric calculations (initializeFlowMetrics), and manages external resources like the Dead Letter Queue (dlqWriter) and Secret Stores."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/ackedqueue/Queue.java",
      "entity_name": "Queue",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 60,
      "line_end": 650,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "The Queue class manages high-level queue logic, low-level page I/O, and data deserialization.",
      "reasoning": "God Class smell -> SRP violation because it mixes high-level queue management (head/tail pages, unread counts) with low-level infrastructure concerns (disk space validation in ensureDiskAvailable, file locking) and data transformation logic (reflection-based deserialization in the deserialize method)."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/common/io/DeadLetterQueueWriter.java",
      "entity_name": "DeadLetterQueueWriter",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 80,
      "line_end": 600,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "DeadLetterQueueWriter manages both the writing of data and the enforcement of retention/storage policies.",
      "reasoning": "Divergent Change smell -> SRP violation because the class has multiple reasons to change: changes to the underlying storage format/writing mechanism, and changes to the administrative policies (age-based retention or size-based storage limits). These policies should be decoupled from the writer implementation."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/FieldReference.java",
      "entity_name": "FieldReference",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 40,
      "line_end": 250,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "FieldReference combines data representation with complex string parsing logic.",
      "reasoning": "Large Class smell -> SRP violation because it encapsulates both the identity of a field path (the data structure) and the complex logic required to parse that path from a string (StrictTokenizer). The parsing logic is a separate responsibility from the representation of the parsed result."
    }
  ]
}

findings.description: The Event class handles data storage, serialization, string interpolation, and memory estimation.
findings.reasoning: God Class smell -> SRP violation because the class encapsulates multiple unrelated responsibilities: it acts as a data carrier (ConvertedMap), handles complex I/O concerns (JSON/CBOR serialization in toJson/fromJson), implements business logic (tagging/timestamp parsing), and provides utility functions like string interpolation (sprintf) and resource management (estimateMemory).

findings.description: AbstractPipelineExt manages pipeline execution, plugin lifecycle, instrumentation, and external resource integration.
findings.reasoning: God Class smell -> SRP violation because it centralizes too many distinct concerns. It manages the pipeline execution state, acts as a coordinator for plugin instantiation (PluginFactory), handles instrumentation and flow metric calculations (initializeFlowMetrics), and manages external resources like the Dead Letter Queue (dlqWriter) and Secret Stores.

findings.description: The Queue class manages high-level queue logic, low-level page I/O, and data deserialization.
findings.reasoning: God Class smell -> SRP violation because it mixes high-level queue management (head/tail pages, unread counts) with low-level infrastructure concerns (disk space validation in ensureDiskAvailable, file locking) and data transformation logic (reflection-based deserialization in the deserialize method).

findings.description: DeadLetterQueueWriter manages both the writing of data and the enforcement of retention/storage policies.
findings.reasoning: Divergent Change smell -> SRP violation because the class has multiple reasons to change: changes to the underlying storage format/writing mechanism, and changes to the administrative policies (age-based retention or size-based storage limits). These policies should be decoupled from the writer implementation.

findings.description: FieldReference combines data representation with complex string parsing logic.
findings.reasoning: Large Class smell -> SRP violation because it encapsulates both the identity of a field path (the data structure) and the complex logic required to parse that path from a string (StrictTokenizer). The parsing logic is a separate responsibility from the representation of the parsed result.
